---
title: "General explore sheds data (2025)"
output: html_notebook
---
Import libraries

```{r}
library(haven)        # Read SPSS files
library(tidyverse)    # Data manipulation and visualization
library(skimr)        # Summary statistics
library(corrplot)     # Correlation plots
library(scales)       # Number formatting
library(ggthemes)     # themes

# Read SPSS file
sheds <- read_sav("/Users/olaf/NAMEfile.sav", encoding="UTF-8")
head(sheds)
```

Create function to apply SPSS labels automatically:

```{r}
# Function to apply SPSS labels automatically
apply_spss_labels <- function(data, variable_name, spss_data) {
  # Get the labels from the original SPSS data
  labels <- attr(spss_data[[variable_name]], "labels")
  
  if (is.null(labels)) {
    # If no labels, return the original values as factors
    return(factor(data[[variable_name]]))
  }
  
  # In SPSS labels, names are the text labels, values are the codes
  # So we need to map from codes (values) to text (names)
  code_to_label <- setNames(names(labels), labels)
  
  # Apply the mapping
  mapped_values <- code_to_label[as.character(data[[variable_name]])]
  
  return(mapped_values)
}
```

Checkout basic metrics:

```{r}
# Convert to tibble and remove labels for easier manipulation
sheds_clean <- sheds %>%
  zap_labels() %>%
  as_tibble()

# Basic overview
glimpse(sheds_clean)
skim(sheds_clean)

# Check completion rate
table(sheds_clean$finished)
prop.table(table(sheds_clean$finished))

# Total number of respondents (all who started)
total_respondents <- nrow(sheds_clean)
cat("Total respondents:", total_respondents, "\n")

# Total finished responses
total_finished <- sum(sheds_clean$finished == 1, na.rm = TRUE)
cat("Finished responses:", total_finished, "\n")

# Completion rate
completion_rate <- total_finished / total_respondents * 100
cat("Completion rate:", round(completion_rate, 1), "%\n")
```

```{r}
# Age distribution using SPSS labels
sheds_clean %>%
  filter(finished == 1) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = apply_spss_labels(., "agegr", sheds))) +
  geom_bar(fill = "steelblue") +
  labs(title = "Age Distribution of Respondents",
       x = "Age Group", y = "Count") +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r}
# Gender distribution using SPSS labels
sheds_clean %>%
  filter(finished == 1) %>%
  count(sex) %>%
  mutate(pct = n/sum(n)*100) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = apply_spss_labels(., "sex", sheds), y = pct)) +
  geom_col(fill = "coral") +
  geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.1f%%", pct)), vjust = -0.5) +
  labs(title = "Gender Distribution",
       x = "Gender", y = "Percentage") +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r}
# Region distribution using SPSS labels
sheds_clean %>%
  filter(finished == 1) %>%
  count(region) %>%
  mutate(region_name = apply_spss_labels(., "region", sheds)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = reorder(region_name, n), y = n)) +
  geom_col(fill = "forestgreen") +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(title = "Regional Distribution",
       x = "Region", y = "Count") +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r}
# Ownership status using SPSS labels
sheds_clean %>%
  filter(finished == 1, !is.na(accom1)) %>%
  count(accom1) %>%
  mutate(status = apply_spss_labels(., "accom1", sheds),
         pct = n/sum(n)*100) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = status, y = pct, fill = status)) +
  geom_col() +
  geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.1f%%", pct)), vjust = -0.5) +
  labs(title = "Housing Ownership Status",
       x = "Status", y = "Percentage") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
```

```{r}
# Renewable devices using SPSS variable labels
device_labels <- c(
  attr(sheds$accom8_1, "label"),
  attr(sheds$accom8_2, "label"), 
  attr(sheds$accom8_3, "label"),
  attr(sheds$accom8_4, "label"),
  attr(sheds$accom8_5, "label")
)

renewable_devices <- sheds_clean %>%
  filter(finished == 1) %>%
  summarise(
    var1 = sum(accom8_1 == 1, na.rm = TRUE),
    var2 = sum(accom8_2 == 1, na.rm = TRUE),
    var3 = sum(accom8_3 == 1, na.rm = TRUE),
    var4 = sum(accom8_4 == 1, na.rm = TRUE),
    var5 = sum(accom8_5 == 1, na.rm = TRUE),
    total = n()
  ) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = -total,
               names_to = "device_type",
               values_to = "count") %>%
  mutate(
    percentage = count/total * 100,
    device_type = factor(device_type,
                        levels = c("var1", "var2", "var3", "var4", "var5"),
                        labels = device_labels)
  )

print(renewable_devices)

ggplot(renewable_devices, aes(x = reorder(device_type, -percentage), y = percentage, fill = device_type)) +
  geom_col() +
  geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.1f%%\n(n=%d)", percentage, count)),
            vjust = -0.5, size = 3.5) +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2") +
  labs(title = "Renewable Energy Devices in Accommodations",
       x = "",
       y = "Percentage of Households") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
```

Heating sources using SPSS labels:
```{r}
heating_sources <- sheds_clean %>%
  filter(finished == 1, !is.na(heat1_1)) %>%
  count(heat1_1) %>%
  mutate(source = apply_spss_labels(., "heat1_1", sheds),
         pct = n/sum(n)*100)

print(heating_sources)

ggplot(heating_sources, aes(x = reorder(source, pct), y = pct)) +
  geom_col(fill = "orangered") +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(title = "Primary Heating Energy Sources",
       x = "Energy Source", y = "Percentage") +
  theme_minimal()
```

Heating temperature:
```{r}
sheds_clean %>%
  filter(finished == 1, heat7 > 0) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = heat7)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 0.5, fill = "coral", color = "black") +
  geom_vline(aes(xintercept = mean(heat7, na.rm = TRUE)),
             color = "red", linetype = "dashed", size = 1) +
  labs(title = "Distribution of Living Room Heating Temperature",
       subtitle = paste("Mean:",
                       round(mean(sheds_clean$heat7, na.rm = TRUE), 1),
                       "°C"),
       x = "Temperature (°C)", y = "Count") +
  theme_minimal()
```

Electricity consumption analysis:

```{r}
elec_stats <- sheds_clean %>%
  filter(finished == 1, elec8_1 > 0, elec8_1 < 50000) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_kwh = mean(elec8_1, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_kwh = median(elec8_1, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_kwh = sd(elec8_1, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_cost = mean(elec7_1, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_cost = median(elec7_1, na.rm = TRUE)
  )

print(elec_stats)

# Electricity cost distribution
sheds_clean %>%
  filter(finished == 1, elec7_1 > 0, elec7_1 < 5000) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = elec7_1)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 50, fill = "gold", color = "black") +
  geom_vline(aes(xintercept = median(elec7_1, na.rm = TRUE)),
             color = "red", linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 1) +
  labs(title = "Distribution of Annual Electricity Costs",
       subtitle = paste("Median:",
                       round(median(sheds_clean$elec7_1, na.rm = TRUE), 0),
                       "CHF"),
       x = "Annual Cost (CHF)", y = "Count") +
  theme_minimal()
```

Car ownership:
```{r}
car_ownership <- sheds_clean %>%
  filter(finished == 1, !is.na(mob2_1)) %>%
  count(mob2_1) %>%
  mutate(pct = n/sum(n)*100)

ggplot(car_ownership, aes(x = factor(mob2_1), y = pct)) +
  geom_col(fill = "navy") +
  geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.1f%%", pct)), vjust = -0.5) +
  labs(title = "Number of Cars per Household",
       x = "Number of Cars", y = "Percentage") +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r}
# Electric vehicle adoption (mob3_3 == 8 means electric)
ev_adoption <- sheds_clean %>%
  filter(finished == 1, mob2_1 > 0) %>%
  summarise(
    total_car_owners = n(),
    ev_owners = sum(mob3_3 == 8 | mob2_e == 1, na.rm = TRUE),
    ev_percentage = ev_owners / total_car_owners * 100
  )

print(ev_adoption)

# Public transport usage with simplified approach
pt_tickets <- sheds_clean %>%
  filter(finished == 1) %>%
  summarise(
    ga_card = sum(mob1_1 > 0, na.rm = TRUE),
    half_fare = sum(mob1_2 > 0, na.rm = TRUE),
    regional = sum(mob1_3 > 0, na.rm = TRUE),
    total = n()
  ) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = -total,
               names_to = "ticket_type",
               values_to = "count") %>%
  mutate(percentage = count/total * 100,
         ticket_type = factor(ticket_type,
                             labels = c("GA Card", "Half-fare", "Regional")))

ggplot(pt_tickets, aes(x = ticket_type, y = percentage)) +
  geom_col(fill = "purple") +
  geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.1f%%", percentage)), vjust = -0.5) +
  labs(title = "Public Transport Ticket Ownership",
       x = "Ticket Type", y = "Percentage of Households") +
  theme_minimal()
```

Airplane travel:
```{r}
# Airplane travel frequency
air_travel <- sheds_clean %>%
  filter(finished == 1) %>%
  mutate(total_flights = (mob13_1 + mob13_2 + mob13_3 + mob13_4)) %>%
  filter(!is.na(total_flights))

ggplot(air_travel, aes(x = total_flights)) +
  geom_bar(fill = "skyblue") +
  labs(title = "Air Travel Frequency (Last 12 Months)",
       x = "Number of Flights", y = "Count") +
  theme_minimal()
```

Climate change concerns using SPSS labels:
```{r}
test_labels <- sheds_clean %>%
  filter(finished == 1, envpsy3 >= 0, !is.na(envpsy3)) %>%
  count(envpsy3) %>%
  mutate(pct = n/sum(n)*100,
         level = apply_spss_labels(., "envpsy3", sheds))

print(test_labels)

sheds_clean %>%
  filter(finished == 1, envpsy3 >= 0, !is.na(envpsy3)) %>%
  count(envpsy3) %>%
  mutate(pct = n/sum(n)*100,
         level = case_when(
           envpsy3 == 1 ~ "Not at all worried",
           envpsy3 == 2 ~ "Slightly worried", 
           envpsy3 == 3 ~ "Moderately worried",
           envpsy3 == 4 ~ "Very worried",
           envpsy3 == 5 ~ "Extremely worried",
           TRUE ~ "Other"
         )) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = level, y = pct)) +
  geom_col(fill = "darkred") +
  geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.1f%%", pct)), vjust = -0.5) +
  labs(title = "Concern About Global Warming",
       x = "Worry Level", y = "Percentage") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
```

Intention to reduce consumption in next 12 months:

```{r}
# Planned reduction in energy consumption (psy8)

reduction_plans <- sheds_clean %>%
  filter(finished == 1) %>%
  summarise(
    electricity = mean(psy8_1, na.rm = TRUE),
    heating = mean(psy8_2, na.rm = TRUE),
    car_usage = mean(psy8_3, na.rm = TRUE),
    flights = mean(psy8_5, na.rm = TRUE),
    carbon_footprint = mean(psy8_4, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  pivot_longer(everything(),
               names_to = "category",
               values_to = "mean_score")

ggplot(reduction_plans, aes(x = reorder(category, mean_score),
                            y = mean_score)) +
  geom_col(fill = "seagreen") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 3, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(title = "Intention to Reduce Consumption (Next 12 Months)",
       subtitle = "Scale: 1=Very unlikely,3=Neiher likely or unlikely 5=Very likely",
       x = "Category", y = "Mean Score") +
  theme_minimal()
```
